description_long: This is a round, heavy-duty manhole cover made primarily of cast iron, featuring a raised, wavy geometric pattern radiating from the center. The surface is partially covered with dry, brown leaves, suggesting it is located outdoors, likely in an urban or park setting. The central area of the cover displays a circular emblem or logo, with some text and symbols visible, indicating possible municipal or utility ownership. The edge of the cover is slightly beveled, and there are no visible bolts or fasteners securing it in place. The cover appears robust and capable of supporting significant weight, with a hard, matte finish and moderate surface roughness. The surrounding ground material is not visible, as the asset is isolated from its environment.
description: A round, cast iron manhole cover with a raised wavy pattern and scattered dry leaves, featuring a central emblem and text.
description_view_0: The manhole cover is seen with a dense cluster of dry leaves along the lower right edge, and the central emblem is partially visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the left edge, where a large piece of debris or leaf extends outward, and the wavy pattern is clearly visible.
description_view_2: The cover is shown with fewer leaves on the left, revealing more of the raised pattern and the central emblem.
description_view_3: The right edge is more exposed, with a concentration of leaves and debris, and the central logo is partially obscured.
attribute_pattern_design: Raised wavy geometric motifs radiate from the center, with a circular emblem and some text in the middle.
